semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all eighteen bounded source mappings, the exact 3/5 proof-carrying, 3/5 tribunal, 2/7 dossier, 1/11 historical method/independence, and related 3/6 epistemic-TCB fixtures, all twenty-one Lean declarations under five public targets, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep made prospective verification-route execution and bounded verdict custody the distinct owner; separated target identity, obligation adequacy, artifact validity, interpretation adequacy, premise/source truth, empirical correspondence, reviewer competence, support, action, rights, cost, and release; required prospective route choice, exact semantic mappings, artifact and TCB boundaries, complete attempt histories, bounded dossiers, multidimensional dependence, adversarial probes, ignorance, dissent, appeal, changed-boundary reuse guards, exact consequences, natural matched baselines, causal ablations, reproduction, and transfer; classified the proof scaffold as twelve predicate-projection or blocking consequences, eight finite route branches, and one assumption-restating authored-summary bridge; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Natural and formal high-value claims and high-risk artifacts submitted for source inspection, citation review, formal proof or countermodel, executable procedure, replay, benchmark, adversarial review, model judging, accountable-human adjudication, appeal, abstention, downgrade, or block, including every attempt, failure, dissent, action, cost, outcome, and residual.
scope.environment: Eighteen inspected assigned sources; 3 valid/5 rejecting authored proof-carrying fixtures; 3 valid/5 rejecting tribunal fixtures; 2 valid/7 rejecting authored dossier cases; one bounded five-project method/independence record and eleven rejecting mutations; a related 3 valid/6 rejecting epistemic-TCB fixture owned by Artifact Graphs; twenty-one finite Lean declarations; and a future frozen natural cross-domain, multi-model, multi-evaluator campaign. No natural verifier-quality or tribunal-quality result is represented.
scope.model: A prospective route-and-verdict contract over frozen targets, eligible modes, interpretation mappings, artifacts, trusted verifier roots, complete attempts, dossiers, reviewer dependence, adversarial probes, stop rules, ignorance, dissent, appeal, verdicts, constraints, expiry, writeback requests, costs, outcomes, and residuals; current implementations are schemas, authored validators, and finite predicates/routes rather than a deployed verification operating system.
scope.authority: Claim Ledgers owns identity/history; Verification Bandwidth owns obligation adequacy; mode-specific formal, source, procedure, replay, benchmark, and empirical evaluators own exact validity; Artifact Graphs owns lineage and receipt faithfulness; Scalable Oversight and accountable humans own competence, appeal, adjudication, and legitimacy; Evidence States owns support; execution owns action; Security, Privacy, Rights, and Licensing own access and use; Resource Economics owns cost; Readiness and Publication own release. This layer owns route execution and bounded verdict custody only and grants no support, action, disclosure, training, or release authority.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; material target, mapping, source, artifact, model, verifier, policy, threat, environment, authority, rights, outcome, consumer, or cost changes expire affected verdicts and prior-review reuse while preserving history.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; eighteen bounded source mappings; three public schemas; proof-carrying, tribunal, adversarial-dossier, historical method/independence, and related epistemic-TCB fixtures; twenty-one declarations across lean/AsiStackProofs/ProofCarryingClaims.lean and lean/AsiStackProofs/Tribunal.lean; future natural target corpus, mappings, proof/source/procedure/replay/benchmark outputs, dossiers, dependence graphs, independent labels, action and appeal traces, delayed outcomes, reproduction locks, and transfer artifacts.

assumptions: High-value claims and high-risk artifacts can be prospectively identified and frozen with an exact consumer consequence before route outcomes.
Eligible route sets and adequacy requests can be specified without choosing methods after seeing favorable results.

Independent-enough evaluators can label natural/formal mappings, source entailment, theorem-target fidelity, artifact validity, attack quality, reviewer dependence, verdict correctness, action follow-through, appeal quality, usefulness, harm, and delayed outcomes.

Direct answer, self-critique, citation-only, proof-only, replay-only, benchmark-only, model-judge, debate, accountable-human, abstention, and governed routes can be matched on information, models, tools, authority, resources, and horizons.

Attempts, failures, timeouts, retries, interventions, dissent, appeals, privacy exposure, human work, costs, missed help, unsafe acceptance, and residuals can be retained closely enough to expose denominator changes.

Artifact validity, semantic adequacy, source truth, empirical correspondence, reviewer competence, support, authority, rights, usefulness, safety, and release remain separable rather than being inferred from route completion.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: A separate verification operating layer adds ceremony, latency, privacy exposure, correlated reviewers, route shopping, false precision, and costly appeal while mature proof assistants, source review, reproducible workflows, benchmark harnesses, human experts, version control, and ordinary release gates already provide the useful checks. No routing record can rescue a wrong formalization, false source, incompetent judge, compromised trusted core, or misleading metric; strong simpler routes may match or dominate the governed layer.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A target, mapping, artifact, trust dependency, attempt, attack, dissent, appeal, required action, cost, outcome, or residual can disappear or silently widen while the record remains conformant; a valid local artifact promotes a semantically invalid broader claim; a required negative result creates an upward effect; or strong matched simpler routes dominate the governed route on joint useful acceptance, false acceptance, false refusal, missed help, interpretation error, attack discovery, calibration, privacy, latency, human burden, and total cost.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze Spinoza, GenesisCode, UAT, Talos, project lineage, PCC, Lean, autoformalization, debate, model-judge, contestability, proof, citation, replay, benchmark, human-review, and abstention comparators without importing source-reported performance.

Justify high-value/high-risk selection, eligible modes, adequacy requests, trusted roots, reviewer and affected-party roles, dossier boundaries, privacy, dissent, veto, ignorance, appeal, adjudication, authority, rights, consequence, and release boundaries.

Specify typed targets, mappings, modes, artifacts, trust delegation, attempts, dependence, attacks, verdicts, consequences, expiry, and countermodels beyond trusted Booleans, authored route order, and fixture summaries.

Run actual proof/source/procedure/replay/benchmark/model-judge/debate/human adapters, artifact checking, dossier assembly, dependence tracking, adversarial probes, action constraints, appeal, writeback, expiry, and replay against natural targets.

Measure mapping error, theorem/source/artifact validity, false acceptance, false refusal, missed help, attack discovery, calibrated ignorance, dissent and appeal preservation, action follow-through, delayed usefulness and harm, privacy, latency, human work, and total cost jointly.

Ablate prospective routing, semantic mapping, artifact binding, trusted roots, complete attempts, dossiers, reviewer dependence, adversarial probes, ignorance, dissent, appeal, reuse guards, and typed writeback under matched tasks and resources.

Reproduce from locks and transfer across models, domains, languages, formal systems, source types, verifier and evaluator implementations, organizations, affected parties, threats, outcome horizons, and time.

contrary_evidence: Proof assistants, PCC-style consumer checking, source and citation review, executable tests, replay systems, benchmark harnesses, debate, model judging, human adjudication, contestability practices, and ordinary release gates already cover important parts without one combined operating layer.
The current fixtures are entirely authored and zero-model: 3/5 proof-carrying, 3/5 tribunal, 2/7 dossier, 1/11 historical method/independence, and a related 3/6 epistemic-TCB record family.

The twenty-one Lean declarations consist of twelve predicate-projection or blocking consequences, eight finite route branches, and one assumption-restating authored-summary bridge; they do not establish semantic or empirical adequacy.

A kernel-valid theorem can target the wrong proposition; an accurate citation can fail to entail the claim; a replay can reproduce a flawed procedure; a benchmark can reward a proxy; a complete tribunal can be consistently wrong.

Debate depends on refutation being easier than deception, trustworthy reveals, judge competence, practical equilibrium, and faithful decomposition; the source's narrow MNIST evidence is not reproduced or transferable here.
Model judges exhibit position, verbosity, self-enhancement, and reasoning biases, while human reviewers and institutions can share incentives, blind spots, or insufficient redress authority.

Additional routing, proof, retrieval, dossier, review, privacy, appeal, and audit work can increase delay, false refusal, missed help, expert burden, opportunity cost, and exposure without improving outcomes.
known_confounds: The governed route may receive more information, tools, verifier compute, review turns, experts, attack budget, appeal opportunity, or latency than baselines.
Target selection may favor formalizable, citation-rich, replayable, or easy-to-review cases and exclude hard failures.

Mappers, proof generators, verifiers, debaters, model judges, critics, human reviewers, and outcome evaluators may share models, data, prompts, code, infrastructure, organizations, incentives, or communication.
Gold mappings, source labels, theorem-target judgments, attack quality, verdict correctness, and affected-party outcomes can be non-unique, culturally narrow, or outcome-informed.
Proof success, citation density, replay fidelity, benchmark score, agreement, confidence, reviewer count, and green validators can be substituted for the target property.

Failed routes, timeouts, abstentions, vetoes, appeals, human interventions, privacy exposure, false refusal, missed help, unsafe acceptance, delayed harm, and residuals can disappear from denominators.
Conservative routing or non-release can reduce observed harm while increasing missed help and making the governed route appear safer.
Short horizons can miss stale proofs, source changes, model drift, policy changes, later contestation, action failure, delayed harm, and appeal outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Every target, route, mapping, artifact, root, dependency, attempt, dossier, reviewer, attack, finding, dissent, appeal, verdict, action, writeback, authority, right, cost, outcome, expiry, and residual has an exact producer, consumer, valid case, rejecting mutation, observed consequence, and replay path.

A prospectively registered natural campaign meets joint mapping, validity, false-acceptance, false-refusal, missed-help, attack-discovery, calibrated-ignorance, dissent/appeal, action, delayed-outcome, privacy, latency, human-work, total-cost, causal, reproduction, and transfer gates against strong matched comparators.

Each claimed mechanism has a predicted causal signature that disappears under matched ablation and cannot be explained by extra resources, target selection, hidden human curation, weak baselines, correlated evaluators, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-release.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact target family, natural and formal wording, assumptions, population, environment, mode, source set, artifact, verifier, policy, evaluator, organization, authority, rights, budget, horizon, outcomes, costs, and residuals.

Separate record conformance, artifact validity, semantic mapping, source truth, theorem validity, empirical correspondence, reviewer competence, verdict correctness, useful action, support, and release.
Unknown mapping, validity, independence, competence, attack coverage, appeal, delayed outcome, cost, reproduction, or transfer remains disputed, residual, narrowed, or blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable ordinary or adversarial trace satisfies its premises and violates target, mapping, artifact, trust, attempt, dossier, dependence, dissent, appeal, verdict, consequence, authority, or denominator preservation.

Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves the joint outcome, the mechanism worsens the frontier, or its effect is explained by resources, selection, curation, weak baselines, evaluator dependence, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-release.

Refute reliable, useful, safer, efficient, contestable, reproducible, or transferable verification-OS language when semantic or validity errors remain hidden, wrong verdicts persist, actions fail, costs dominate, a simpler comparator wins, or reproduction and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise adequacy, proof, source-review, artifact, oversight, human-governance, evidence, execution, or release owner supersedes the atom and every target, attempt, verdict, dependent, action, appeal, surface, consumer, and residual migrates with preserved lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and execute the natural multi-route campaign with accepted mappings and validity labels, actual proof/source/procedure/replay/benchmark/model-judge/debate/human routes, strong matched comparators, multidimensional dependence and appeal, complete failures and costs, causal ablations, clean reproduction, and transfer across a second model, domain, language, formal system, evaluator, organization, threat, and time period.

non_claims: A schema-valid record, proof term, citation, replay, benchmark, reviewer label, majority verdict, independence-group count, green route theorem, or complete dossier does not establish target meaning, truth, adequacy, competence, usefulness, safety, support, authority, redress, or release.
The current authored fixtures and twenty-one declarations establish only their exact finite record, route, negative-control, and no-promotion boundaries.

No claim identity, adequacy, formal validity, source truth, empirical result, reviewer competence, support, action, rights, release, publication, AGI, or ASI state changes through this semantic review.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Existing mode-specific verifiers and accountable human review already cover this boundary without a combined operating layer.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: No unique exercised consumer or failure remains after owner decomposition, or a matched natural campaign shows added routing and review cost without joint outcome improvement.
acceptance_criteria: Demonstrate natural routing quality, bounded verdict correctness, useful downstream effects, complete cost, causal contribution, reproduction, and transfer.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: A competent reviewer can select the obvious verification method without a separate protocol.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Strong ordinary workflows preserve target, mapping, attempts, failures, dissent, constraints, and scope with no material laundering cases.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare cases where route, mapping, artifact, trust, or verdict failure changes a support, action, or release decision.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Proof assistants, citations, replay, tests, benchmarks, and human review compose cleanly without this layer.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: A matched composed baseline preserves every claimed boundary and equals or dominates the governed route.
acceptance_criteria: Implement and compare the strongest composed baseline rather than weak isolated methods.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Schemas, fixtures, and finite routes are not a verifier or tribunal implementation.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Any stated fixture count, declaration classification, artifact path, ownership boundary, or non-claim is inaccurate or presented as natural evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Keep the 3/5, 3/5, 2/7, 1/11, related 3/6, and 21-declaration boundaries exact and require natural execution before promotion.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: The proposal combines established formal verification, evaluation, debate, and review practices without measurable novelty.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: The governed route fails any preregistered usefulness, error, calibration, appeal, cost, causal, reproduction, or transfer gate against strong matched comparators.
acceptance_criteria: Freeze exact comparators and thresholds before outcomes and report positive, negative, null, narrowed, refuted, and cost-dominated results.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: A sufficiently trusted verifier can directly determine support and action.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Any route completion silently collapses semantic adequacy, validity, verdict, support, authority, or release without an owning decision.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile every state and consequence to its exact producer, owner, evidence, decision, and expiry receipt.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Readers and downstream systems can infer reasonable generalization from a narrow result.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: A wider proposition, population, environment, policy, threat, outcome, or time inherits standing without separate mapping and evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Use adversarial scope mutations and independently audit every inferred widening and consequence.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Standard proof kernels, signatures, model providers, and CI already supply adequate roots of trust.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: A verifier or trust delegate authenticates itself, recursion lacks a stop, compromise has no response, or outside-TCB residuals disappear.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise root loss, delegated compromise, stale versions, recursive verification, independent observation, and recovery on actual artifacts.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Different models or reviewer roles are independent enough in practice.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Shared dependence inflates effective reviewer count or recorded separation is used as competence evidence without calibration.
acceptance_criteria: Measure dependence and error correlation across model, data, prompt, code, infrastructure, organization, incentives, time, access, and communication.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Majority verdict and confidence capture all decision-relevant uncertainty.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Counterexamples, omissions, bias probes, ignorance, abstention, veto, minority findings, or appeal grounds vanish or are converted into agreement.
acceptance_criteria: Trace every negative and contestability state through verdict, action, downstream use, and delayed outcome.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Review reuse by claim ID and evidence digest is sufficient.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Changed mapping, source, artifact, model, verifier, policy, threat, environment, authority, rights, or outcome reuses a prior verdict as current.
acceptance_criteria: Mutate every boundary dimension and test invalidation, re-review, replay, and residual handling.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: A passed trusted route should automatically promote a claim or unblock execution.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Any verifier, tribunal, judge, proof bridge, fixture, route function, receipt, or reviewer creates its own upward support, action, disclosure, training, or release effect.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile every effect to a separate accepted owner decision with exact target, scope, evidence, authority, and residuals.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Only final accepted verdicts matter to system value.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Restoring failed routes, timeouts, retries, interventions, dissent, appeals, privacy, human burden, false refusal, missed help, unsafe acceptance, delay, or residuals materially changes no conclusion.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile target-to-outcome histories with total resource, privacy, recovery, human, appeal, and opportunity costs.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Modern autoformalizers plus human spot checks make material mapping errors rare.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Adversarial quantifier, assumption, scope, and ontology mutations never yield checked but wrong targets under the frozen campaign.
acceptance_criteria: Measure mapping precision, recall, disagreement, counterexample consequences, and independent correction cost.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Using multiple routes until one succeeds is ordinary robustness.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Post-outcome route changes, optional stopping, selective combination, and selective reporting never alter standing or reported error.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-register route portfolios and report all attempts, stopping decisions, multiplicity, and counterfactual route outcomes.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Strong model judges and trained human reviewers resist superficial persuasion well enough.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Position, verbosity, style, confidence, familiarity, rhetoric, and strategic evidence selection never change verdicts holding target evidence fixed.
acceptance_criteria: Run randomized order/style/evidence-reveal controls with human calibration and delayed truth labels.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Stable repeated adversarial agreement is strong practical evidence.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Edit stability, repeated agreement, and cycle caps never coexist with known false targets, hidden attacks, or judge failure.
acceptance_criteria: Seed unresolved counterexamples and vary budgets, turns, judges, and attack access while measuring false stable convergence.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem provides meaningful proof that formal tiers carry justification.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: The predicate's trusted premise or Boolean artifact reference is presented as artifact validity, target fidelity, or runtime enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: Replace or supplement with typed artifact identities, checker semantics, countermodels, and implementation refinement before substantive classification.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem proves failed verification blocks promotion.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: No runtime or ledger implementation is connected, or a failed real route still creates an upward effect.
acceptance_criteria: Prove refinement to an executable owner-gated transition system and test observed effects under faults and retries.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: Required roles, evidence refs, and dissent are sufficient formal assurance for tribunal verdicts.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: A record satisfies the predicate while reviewers are dependent, evidence false, dissent meaningless, or verdict wrong.
acceptance_criteria: Add typed evidence and dependence semantics plus countermodels and connect to calibrated natural review outcomes.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem proves high-risk missing review blocks acceptance.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: The Boolean policy premise lacks runtime authority or a real high-risk artifact bypasses the modeled route.
acceptance_criteria: Connect risk policy and tribunal presence to executable admission and independently observed denial effects.

spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.strongest_counterclaim: The bridge formalizes the dossier probe's substantive success.
spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-.falsifier: Any conjunct is merely asserted, mismatches the JSON artifact, or is narrated as semantic equivalence, judge quality, verdict correctness, or support evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Bind generated data to typed semantics and prove artifact correspondence before classifying above an authored-summary bridge.

prose_review_lineage.retirement_reason: The chapter proof surface was rewritten during the active refinement; this sentence is no longer current prose. Listed replacements preserve the nearest current semantic role, not textual identity.
